title: South Boston runner supports BMC social workers in Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Paige Beattie, 23, from South Boston, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team BMC.
Beattie is fundraising to support Boston Medical Center's safety-net mission and social workers helping patients in crisis.
She aims to raise $10,000 through events, with all donations enhancing BMC's patient care capabilities.

title: Patience remains an elusive virtue for Legacy FC attack
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC lost 1-0 to San Diego Wave, remaining winless in their NWSL debut season.
Coach Filipa Patão emphasized patience, targeting improvement in the team's rushed performance in the final third.
Striker Amanda Gutierres, despite involvement, failed to score in four matches, struggling to connect passes effectively.

title: Melrose man charged with stealing hundreds of thousands from clients, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Henry Rappa Jr. of Melrose is charged with embezzling hundreds of thousands from clients and practicing law while suspended.
Rappa allegedly misused $660,000 entrusted by a client and spent money from trust accounts despite court orders.
Rappa was ordered to surrender his passport; his bail was set at $2,500 after arraignment in Malden District Court.

title: Eugene Mirman posts message after fiery NH crash and dramatic rescue
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Comedian Eugene Mirman was rescued from a burning vehicle after crashing near Bedford Toll Plaza, New Hampshire.
Mirman thanked rescuers and hospital staff in a social media post, expressing gratitude and humor about his recovery.
The incident is under investigation with no charges filed; police seek information from the public.

title: NH trooper assaulted in unprovoked attack
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 53-year-old Dover man allegedly assaulted a NH State Trooper in an unprovoked attack on Dover Point Road.
Jonathan G. Newcomb faces felony charges including criminal threatening and attempting to take a firearm from law enforcement.
Both the trooper and Newcomb received minor injuries, were treated at Wentworth-Douglass Hospital, and later released.

title: This runner ‘barely finished’ the Boston Marathon last year. Now he’s back to run again.
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Bryan Mallett, 24, is running the Boston Marathon to support Mass General after losing a close family member to cancer.
Mallett's late second mother, Sharla Sieve, inspired a successful fundraiser for Mass General's new hepatic arterial infusion program.
After a challenging finish last year, Mallett aims to honor Sieve's legacy by running the marathon for a second consecutive year.

title: NFL executives reportedly ‘expect’ Patriots to acquire A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: NFL executives anticipate Patriots to acquire Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown after June 1 due to salary-cap considerations.
The trade interest between the Patriots and A.J. Brown links to head coach Mike Vrabel's connections, despite no official deal.
Eagles reportedly want at least a first-round draft pick for Brown, with New England monitoring the trade conditions closely.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: '28 Years Later: The Bone Temple,' directed by Nia DaCosta, is now streaming on Netflix.
'The Testament of Ann Lee,' starring Amanda Seyfried, is available on Hulu.
Ken Burns' documentary 'Henry David Thoreau,' featuring Jeff Goldblum, streams on PBS.

title: Caleb Durbin’s redemption, Willson Contreras’s swagger & other takeaways from Red Sox’ home-opening win
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Willson Contreras and Marcelo Mayer homered as the Red Sox won their home opener against the Padres 5-2 at Fenway Park.
Caleb Durbin broke a slump with an RBI single, ending an 0-for-34 streak dating back to his time with the Brewers.
Pitcher Johan Oviedo will be placed on the 15-day injured list due to an elbow strain, undergoing further evaluation.

title: Here are the dates for the Patriots’ offseason workout schedule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots begin phase one of offseason workouts on April 20, focusing on conditioning and meetings.
OTAs for the Patriots are scheduled for May 27, May 29, June 1-2, June 4, June 8-9, and June 11.
The offseason program concludes with a mandatory minicamp from June 15-17, before training camp starts in July.
